title: hieracium subpiliferum
intro: Hieracium subpiliferum, also known as Pilosella subpilifera, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits of Hieracium subpiliferum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, making it a popular remedy for stomach ulcers and gastritis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment for skin irritations and wounds.
